name: describealign

metadata.name: describealign
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: Combines videos with matching audio files (e.g. audio descriptions)

documentation:
For usage help, simply run the script directly.
If the Scripts folder has been added to PATH, can be run
directly from console with "describealign" in any directory.
The github contains more usage information.

modules.name: describealign
documentation:

combines videos with matching audio files (e.g. audio descriptions)
input: video or folder of videos and an audio file or folder of audio files
output: videos in a folder "videos_with_ad", with aligned segments of the audio replaced
this script aligns the new audio to the video using the video's old audio
first, the video's sound and the audio file are both converted to spectrograms
second, the two spectrograms are roughly aligned by finding their longest common subsequence
third, the rough alignment is denoised through L1-Minimization
fourth, the spectrogram alignments determine where the new audio replaces the old

functions.name: describealign.parse_audio_from_file
documentation: read audio from file with ffmpeg and convert to numpy array

functions.name: describealign.tokenize_audio
documentation: tokenize audio by transforming with a mel-frequency cepstrum (MFC)

functions.name: describealign.tokenize_audio_dither
documentation: same as tokenize_audio, but dithering the MFC window timings
this allows for finer alignment by ameliorating discretization error

functions.name: describealign.normalize_spec
documentation: normalize along both time and frequency axes to allow comparing tokens by correlation

functions.name: describealign.rough_align
documentation: vectorized implementation of the Wagner–Fischer (Longest Common Subsequence) algorithm
modified to include affine gap penalties and skip+match options (i.e. knight's moves)
gaps are necessary when parts are cut out of the audio description (e.g. cut credits)
or when the audio description includes a commercial break or an extra scene
the skip+match option allows for micro-adjustments without eating the full gap penalty
skip+match is primarily useful in maintaining alignment when the rates differ slightly

functions.name: describealign.chunk_path
documentation: chunk path segments of similar slope into clips
a clip has the form: (start_index, end_index)

functions.name: describealign.smooth_align
documentation: find piece-wise linear alignment that minimizes the weighted combination of
total absolute error at each node and total absolute slope change of the fit
distance between nodes and the fit (i.e. errors) are weighted by node quality
absolute slope changes are differences between the slopes of adjacent fit lines
slope changes are weighted much more than node errors to smooth out noise
the main source of noise is rough alignment drift while the describer is speaking

functions.name: describealign.cap_synced_end_points
documentation: if the start or end were marked as synced during smooth alignment then
extend that alignment to the edge (i.e. to the start/end of the audio)

functions.name: describealign.plot_alignment
documentation: visualize both the rough and smooth alignments

functions.name: describealign.replace_aligned_segments
documentation: use the smooth alignment to replace runs of video sound with corresponding described audio

functions.name: describealign.detect_describer
documentation: identify which segments of the replaced audio actually have the describer speaking
uses a Naive Bayes classifier smoothed with L1-Minimization to identify the describer

functions.name: describealign.encode_fit_as_ffmpeg_expr
documentation: Convert piece-wise linear fit to ffmpeg expression for editing video frame timestamps

functions.name: describealign.write_replaced_media_to_disk
documentation: outputs a new media file with the replaced audio (which includes audio descriptions)

functions.name: describealign.is_ffmpeg_installed
documentation: check whether static_ffmpeg has already installed ffmpeg and ffprobe

functions.name: describealign.combine
documentation: combines videos with matching audio files (e.g. audio descriptions)
this is the main function of this script, it calls the other functions in order

functions.name: describealign.migrate_config
documentation:
Migrate configuration from old location.

Only runs if the old_path exists but new_path does not

functions.name: describealign.command_line_interface
documentation: Entry point for command line interaction, for example:
> describealign video.mp4 audio_desc.mp3
